violations.description: Date marking and disposition of ready-to-eat potentially hazardous food. — --Refrigerated, ready-to-eat potentially hazardous food (prepared more than 24 hours ago) was not properly date marked. - Multiple containers of food made several days prior found in refrigerators and walk-in cooler without dates or labels.

Contaminated food disposition. — --Unsafe, adulterated or contaminated food was not discarded as required. - Box of tomatoes in walk-in cooler has some tomatoes that are not eatable and needs to be discarded.

Clean-in-place (CIP) equipment - Design and construction. — --Food contact surface of clean in place equipment was not cleaned properly. - Wire shelves in walk-in cooler rusty and collecting food buildup. In need of cleaning.
Ventilation hood systems - Design and construction. — --Ventilation hood system was not provided over all cooking equipment. - Hood is covering the fryer but not the stove.

Plumbing system. — --Plumbing system was not designed, constructed, operated and/or maintained according to regulation. -There is a pipe that is leaking in the basement that is in need of repair. The leak is running into a funnel currently and draining into a bucket. -The walk-in cooler condensation is draining into a bucket. Walk-in coolers need to be drained to the outside and not into buckets.

Handling and storage of poisonous and toxic materials. — --Secondary container of poisonous or toxic material was not clearly identified to indicated contents. - Spray bottle by handwashing sink not labeled to identify contents.
